title: Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --token-auth-file parameter is not set
input_row.description: Do not use token based authentication.
input_row.rationale: The token-based authentication utilizes static tokens to authenticate requests to the apiserver. The tokens are stored in clear-text in a file on the apiserver, and cannot be revoked or rotated without restarting the apiserver. Hence, do not use static token- based authentication. Impact: You will have to configure and use alternate authentication mechanisms such as certificates. Static token based authentication could not be used.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --token-auth-file argument does not exist. Alternative Audit Method kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[ ]}{.spec.containers[ ].command} {"\n"}{end}' | grep '--token-auth-file' | grep -i false If the exit code is '1', then the control isn't present / failed
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and configure alternate mechanisms for authentication. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and remove the --token-auth- file=<filename> parameter. Default Value: By default, --token-auth-file argument is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authentication/#static-token-file 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/
input_row.impact: You will have to configure and use alternate authentication mechanisms such as certificates. Static token based authentication could not be used.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_token_auth_file_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification in the kube-system namespace using the Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- Use the following command to extract the command-line arguments for the kube-apiserver containers:
  ```
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath="{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command}{'\n'}{end}"
  ```
- Parse the output to check for the presence of the --token-auth-file flag.
- Pass Criteria: The --token-auth-file argument is NOT present in any kube-apiserver container command.
- Fail Criteria: The --token-auth-file argument IS present in any kube-apiserver container command.
- This check can be fully automated using Kubernetes APIs or kubectl without requiring host-level access or subjective review.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of this control as Automated is correct. The check can be performed using Kubernetes APIs or the `kubectl` command-line tool without requiring manual intervention.
- The use of `kubectl` to query the kube-apiserver Pod specification and check for the presence of the `--token-auth-file` parameter is appropriate and aligns with Kubernetes CLI capabilities.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically correct. The use of `kubectl get pods` with the appropriate JSONPath query is a valid method to extract the command-line arguments of the kube-apiserver containers.
- The command provided is accurate and should effectively identify the presence or absence of the `--token-auth-file` parameter.
- The program name `k8s_api_server_token_auth_file_disabled` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, though it could be slightly improved for clarity.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `k8s_apiserver_no_token_auth_file` to more clearly indicate that the check is ensuring the absence of the token auth file parameter.
- Consider adding a check for multiple kube-apiserver instances, as clusters may have more than one instance for high availability.
- It might be beneficial to include a note on the potential impact of removing the `--token-auth-file` parameter, such as ensuring alternative authentication methods are configured.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded and uses appropriate methods to automate the compliance check. The technical details are accurate, and the approach is consistent with Kubernetes security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify
- Modify the program name to `k8s_apiserver_no_token_auth_file` for improved clarity.
- Ensure the assessment considers clusters with multiple kube-apiserver instances and checks each one.
- Keep the rest of the assessment as-is, as the automated approach and technical details are sound.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that this control can be effectively audited using Kubernetes APIs or the `kubectl` command-line tool without requiring manual intervention. The audit steps provided in the compliance control data can be executed automatically, making it feasible to check for the presence of the `--token-auth-file` parameter in the kube-apiserver configuration.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
k8s_apiserver_no_token_auth_file

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Kubernetes Resource/Service: Pods
- API/CLI Method: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath="{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command}{'\n'}{end}"`
- Properties to Check: Command-line arguments of kube-apiserver containers
- Pass Criteria: The `--token-auth-file` argument is NOT present in any kube-apiserver container command.
- Fail Criteria: The `--token-auth-file` argument IS present in any kube-apiserver container command.
